workflow_name: Internal Account Review Transcript -> Team Actions
user_message: Give me the manager view on this Adatum Corporation escalation email.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: competitive positioning Discussion - Adatum Corporation
Date: 2024-02-08
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation), Raj Mehta (Microsoft), David (Adatum Corporation)

[00:02:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on competitive positioning from our last session.

[00:08:00] David (Adatum Corporation): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:10:00] David (Adatum Corporation): The timeline for data residency is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:14:00] Laura Nguyen (Adatum Corporation): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:18:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Raj Mehta (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

handoffs.condition: team coaching or risk review input

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF52-J trap (Follow-up cadence generic not tied to specific actions); produces complete Team action items with owners, coaching notes (constructive frame), customer-facing draft (sanitized
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF52-J failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF52-J failure mode (Follow-up cadence generic not tied to specific actions)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF52-J -- Follow-up cadence generic not tied to specific actions
